skill.name: product-strategist
skill.description: Strategic product leadership toolkit for OKR cascade generation, market analysis, vision setting, and team scaling.
skill.summary: Generate product strategy OKRs and alignment views from company goals.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns and one shell backtick pattern. Review found these are false positives in documentation, generated report text, and string replacement logic. The skill includes a local Python script, but no malicious intent, network access, file writes, subprocess calls, or prompt injection evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alerts
low_findings.description: The static alerts match product strategy text and Python string replacement, not cryptographic algorithms. Duplicate alerts were present in generated report content and documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited script line maps product objective wording through string data, and the other cited lines are markdown or JSON text. No cryptographic API or weak algorithm use was found at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive Shell Backtick Alert
low_findings.description: The backtick alert is a markdown usage example for running the bundled Python script. It is documentation, not automatic Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is markdown inline code around a command example. The script itself does not use subprocess, shell execution, or dynamic code execution.

low_findings.title: Bundled Local Script Requires Normal Review
low_findings.description: The skill includes a Python script that reads command-line arguments and prints generated OKRs. No unsafe behavior was found, but executable scripts are a minor marketplace risk factor.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script has a clear command-line entry point and only uses local computation and stdout. The residual risk is from executable code presence, not observed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Product leaders need strategy artifacts that connect company goals to team execution. This skill generates OKR cascades, alignment scores, and dashboard views for planning reviews.
actual_capabilities: Generates company OKRs from growth, retention, revenue, innovation, and operational templates.
Cascades company objectives into product-level objectives with contribution targets.
Creates team-level OKRs for Growth, Platform, Mobile, and Data teams.
Calculates vertical alignment, horizontal alignment, coverage, balance, and overall scores.
Prints a readable OKR dashboard and can print a JSON export when requested.
limitations: Uses fixed templates and default sample metrics unless the script is edited.
Does not connect to Jira, Asana, spreadsheets, or OKR tracking systems.
Does not persist generated OKRs or track progress across quarters.
Uses simple keyword matching for team relevance and alignment scoring.

use_cases.title: Quarterly Strategy Planning
use_cases.description: Create a first OKR cascade for leadership review before team planning starts.

use_cases.title: Team Objective Drafting
use_cases.description: Translate product priorities into team objectives and key results for planning sessions.

use_cases.title: Alignment Review
use_cases.description: Review how company objectives connect to product work and team focus areas.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic OKRs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use product-strategist to generate growth OKRs and summarize the company, product, and team objectives.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Strategy Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use product-strategist to compare growth, retention, and revenue OKR cascades for our next quarter.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Team Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use product-strategist to create team-level OKRs for Growth, Platform, Mobile, and Data teams.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Strategic Alignment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use product-strategist to generate a revenue OKR cascade, explain the alignment scores, and identify weak coverage areas.

output_examples.input: Generate growth-focused OKRs for the next quarter.
output: Company objectives focus on acquisition, product-market fit, and sustainable growth.
Product objectives translate those goals into viral features, hypothesis validation, and product-led growth loops.
Team objectives assign relevant work to Growth, Platform, Mobile, and Data teams.
Alignment scoring summarizes vertical alignment, horizontal alignment, coverage, balance, and overall focus.

output_examples.input: Create revenue strategy OKRs and explain team contributions.
output: The revenue cascade emphasizes monetization, revenue per customer, and churn reduction.
Product work focuses on pricing, premium features, and product-driven revenue.
Team contributions show how each default team supports revenue goals.

output_examples.input: Review the alignment quality of a generated cascade.
output: The dashboard lists company, product, and team objectives in hierarchy order.
The alignment matrix shows which lower-level objectives support each company goal.
The score summary highlights coverage gaps and uneven team balance.

best_practices: Start with the strategy template that matches the current business priority.
Review generated objectives with leaders before using them in team commitments.
Adjust metrics, teams, and wording to match the real organization.
anti_patterns: Treating generated OKRs as final without leadership and team review.
Using default sample metrics as real business targets.
Ignoring low alignment or balance scores during planning.

faq.question: What strategy templates are included?
faq.answer: The script includes growth, retention, revenue, innovation, and operational templates.

faq.question: Can it customize teams?
faq.answer: The default script uses Growth, Platform, Mobile, and Data teams. Custom teams require editing the script.

faq.question: Does it integrate with planning tools?
faq.answer: No. It prints a dashboard and can print a JSON export, but it does not call external tools.

faq.question: How are alignment scores calculated?
faq.answer: The script scores vertical alignment, horizontal alignment, coverage, balance, and an overall weighted score.

faq.question: Is this safe to run?
faq.answer: The reviewed script uses local computation and stdout. It does not use network calls, file writes, or shell execution.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
